title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray secured his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox player, contributing to a 5-3 victory.
Gray pitched 6.1 innings, allowing eight hits, three runs, and two walks, with his performance involving 79 pitches.
Gray, who disliked his time with the Yankees in 2017-18, has a 7-1 record with a 3.20 ERA this season.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer and went 2 for 4 with 3 RBIs in Red Sox win.
Contreras showed frustration after colliding with Andruw Monasterio, leading to a dugout outburst.
Interim manager Chad Tracy praises Contreras for his leadership, work ethic, and influence on younger players.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with hail and wind expected in Massachusetts Saturday evening, per NWS.
Greatest severe weather risk is west and northwest of Interstate 95, affecting much of Southern New England.
Storms could cause localized flooding and damage to vehicles and property between 4 p.m. and 10 p.m.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased patrols at Nantasket Beach Friday due to social media rumors of a 'takeover' event.
Despite concerns, no incidents occurred at Nantasket Beach, and it was quieter with ample parking available.
State troopers reported no gathering or arrests at the nearby Carson Beach, another rumored location.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle embraces an expressive style on the mound, using emotion to boost team momentum.
Tolle credits his late mother for encouraging him to 'play with joy', honoring her memory in his performances.
Tolle's humorous postgame interviews highlight his unique personality, providing entertainment despite Boston's challenging season.

title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd criticized the Boston Celtics for salary cap constraints due to Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown's contracts.
Cowherd compared Celtics to Knicks, highlighting New York's financial flexibility thanks to Jalen Brunson's team-friendly contract.
Cowherd emphasized Knicks' cohesive play and salary structure as advantages over the Celtics' duo-dependent strategy.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Pixar's 'Hoppers,' featuring a tech-savvy beaver, is now available on Disney+.
'Pillion,' an unconventional romcom about a BDSM relationship, is streaming on HBO Max.
Apple TV's 'Cape Fear' series, starring Amy Adams, adapts John D. MacDonald’s novel.

title: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to 60-day IL as ace continues to deal with discomfort
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox placed Garrett Crochet on the 60-day IL due to persistent lat tightness from a previous injury.
Reliever Joe La Sorsa was added to the 40-man roster, filling the spot opened by Crochet's move.
Pitcher Brayan Bello is demoted to Triple-A Worcester amid struggles amid Boston's ongoing rotation adjustments.

title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College baseball lost to Denison University 4-3 in the Division III National Championship Series Game 3.
The Gulls forced a decisive Game 3 with a thrilling 11-10 victory in extra innings during Game 2.
Endicott's appearance in the national championship was the baseball program's first, marking its most successful season.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket resident Meghan Perry brought laxative-laced brownies to a School Committee meeting, aiming to critique PFAS in synthetic turf.
School Committee member Laura Gallagher Byrne called the gesture a provocative display intended to ridicule the committee.
No one consumed the brownies, and a police report was filed; it's unclear if Perry will face charges.
